Description: Modeling complex dependency structures between multiple variables, allowing for the isolation of marginal distributions from their dependence framework.
Focusing on high-magnitude events that exceed specific risk thresholds, utilizing the Generalized Pareto Distribution (GPD) for accurate tail-risk estimation.
Quantifying the clustering of extreme precipitation events over time to adjust standard extreme value distributions for temporal dependence.
Leveraging Bayes' theorem to borrow strength across different spatial locations, providing rigorous uncertainty quantification for parameter estimates.
Updating Intensity-Duration-Frequency parameters for non-stationary climates to accurately predict the probability of future flood events.
